title: The Agent updates the Close Blocker Triage Log entry for the FX revaluation drift blocker (record airtable_4d0cdf5258b5, tied to exception exc_cb0a9a94a3084c).
justification: The prompt says 'square the triage record away so it reflects the true status.' This rubric confirms the Agent actually updated the correct triage entry --the one tied to the FX revaluation drift exception exc_cb0a9a94a3084c.
evidence: Look for an update to the Close Blocker Triage Log record airtable_4d0cdf5258b5 (the entry referencing exception exc_cb0a9a94a3084c) in the 'Close Blocker Triage Log' table. Confirm the update tool call returned success.

title: The Agent's triage-log update corrects the GL account to '132000 Prepaid Facilities Maintenance' (replacing the incorrect '131000 Prepaid Software Subscriptions').
justification: The prompt says the triage detail 'doesn't even line up' and asks the Agent to make the record reflect the true status. The real exception is on account 132000 Prepaid Facilities Maintenance, not the 131000 Prepaid Software Subscriptions wrongly listed on the entry.
evidence: Check the parameters of the triage-log update on record airtable_4d0cdf5258b5. The gl_account should be corrected to '132000 Prepaid Facilities Maintenance', replacing the incorrect '131000 Prepaid Software Subscriptions'.

title: The Agent's triage-log update corrects the reconciliation reference to 'BL-3978BDB68290' (replacing the incorrect archived 'BL-3E4ED5B5B9BA').
justification: The prompt asks the Agent to square the record to the true status. The reconciliation actually behind this exception is BL-3978BDB68290 (FP-2026-05, account 132000); the entry wrongly references BL-3E4ED5B5B9BA, an archived FP-2025-09 recon on account 131000.
evidence: Check the parameters of the triage-log update on record airtable_4d0cdf5258b5. The recon_id should be corrected to 'BL-3978BDB68290', replacing the incorrect archived 'BL-3E4ED5B5B9BA'.

title: The Agent's triage-log update reflects that the blocker is still open / unresolved and awaiting partner sign-off from James Randall (not marked resolved or approved).
justification: The prompt says 'If it turns out nobody's actually resolved it, square the triage record away so it reflects the true status.' The exception is still in awaiting_approval, so the record must show it remains open and pending James Randall's sign-off.
evidence: Check the parameters of the triage-log update on record airtable_4d0cdf5258b5 (status and/or notes fields). The update should reflect that the blocker is still open / unresolved and awaiting partner sign-off from James Randall -- not resolved or approved.

title: The Agent notifies George McAdam (george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com) about the open blocker.
justification: The prompt says 'make sure George knows it's still open and waiting on James's sign-off so he can get it over the line.' This rubric confirms the Agent actually reached George. The prompt names the goal (notify) not a channel, so any valid channel to George satisfies it.
evidence: Look for a notification to george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com (email, Slack DM, or other message). Confirm the write action returned success.

title: The Agent's notification to George McAdam identifies the exception (exc_cb0a9a94a3084c) and the underlying reconciliation (BL-3978BDB68290).
justification: The prompt asks the Agent to make sure George knows about this specific blocker. George needs the system identifiers (exception_id and recon_id) to find and act on the item in BlackLine.
evidence: Check the content of the notification to George McAdam. It should name both exception exc_cb0a9a94a3084c and reconciliation BL-3978BDB68290.

title: The Agent's notification to George McAdam identifies the affected account (132000 Prepaid Facilities Maintenance) and the FX revaluation drift amount (approximately $435.41).
justification: Beyond the system IDs, George needs to know the accounting substance of the blocker - which account is affected and the size of the variance - to gauge materiality and brief James.
evidence: Check the content of the notification to George McAdam. It should name account 132000 Prepaid Facilities Maintenance and the FX revaluation drift amount of approximately $435.41.

title: The Agent's notification to George McAdam states the blocker is still open / unresolved and that the outstanding step is James Randall's partner sign-off.
justification: The prompt says to let George know 'it's still open and waiting on James's sign-off.' The notification must communicate both that the item is unresolved and that James Randall's approval is the outstanding gate.
evidence: Check the content of the notification to George McAdam. It should state the item is still open / unresolved and that the only outstanding step is James Randall's partner sign-off.

title: The Agent's notification to George McAdam indicates George should chase James Randall to get the partner sign-off completed so the item does not slip into the next close cycle.
justification: The prompt says George should 'get it over the line rather than letting it slip into the next cycle.' The notification must prompt George to act on James's sign-off.
evidence: Check the content of the notification to George McAdam. It should ask or indicate that George should chase/pursue James Randall to get the sign-off done so the item doesn't slip into the next close cycle.

title: The Agent reports that exception exc_cb0a9a94a3084c was never signed off and is still pending (awaiting_approval).
justification: The prompt asks directly 'whether that exception was ever signed off or it's still pending.' The exception state is awaiting_approval (never approved/closed), so the Agent must report it as still pending.
evidence: Check the Agent's final response. It should report that exception exc_cb0a9a94a3084c was never signed off and remains pending - in the awaiting_approval state.

title: The Agent identifies James Randall as the approver on exception exc_cb0a9a94a3084c.
justification: Identifying who the approver is matters because the user's downstream ask is for George to chase the right partner. James Randall is the named approver on the exception.
evidence: Check the Agent's final response. It should name James Randall as the approver on exception exc_cb0a9a94a3084c.

title: The Agent reports that reconciliation BL-3978BDB68290 has not moved since it went in_progress on 2026-05-31 (still unreviewed and unsubmitted).
justification: The prompt asks 'whether the reconciliation sitting behind it has moved at all or it's exactly where it was.' The recon has been in_progress since 2026-05-31 with no reviewer, no submission, and no certification - exactly where it was.
evidence: Check the Agent's final response. It should report that reconciliation BL-3978BDB68290 has not progressed since it went in_progress on 2026-05-31 - still unreviewed and unsubmitted (reviewer/submitted null).

title: The Agent reports that James Randall never responded to Harry Marks's approval request (the request was left hanging with no reply).
justification: The prompt asks 'whether James ever responded to Harry or it's just been left hanging.' Harry's June 3 approval-request email to James has no reply anywhere, confirming James left it unanswered.
evidence: Check the Agent's final response. It should report that James Randall never responded to Harry Marks's approval request - there is no reply from James, so the request was left hanging.

title: The Agent reports that Jones Harrison initially advised treating the $435.41 as acceptable translation noise (no corrective JE).
justification: The prompt explicitly asks 'whether Jones and Harry were genuinely on the same page... because I've a feeling that got muddled.' The muddle's first beat is Jones's initial position - that the variance should be treated as translation noise with no corrective JE. Capturing this opening position is required to explain what later changed.
evidence: Check the Agent's final response. It should report Jones's initial position - that the $435.41 should be treated as acceptable translation noise with no corrective JE (or similar).

title: The Agent reports that Jones Harrison later reversed himself and concluded a corrective JE was the right call.
justification: The second beat of the muddle is Jones's reversal at 13:25 ('Corrective JE is the right call here') because the GBP 33K London prepaid block amortizes through January 2027 and the May FX move created the drift. Reporting Jones's reversal is what explains why the settled position differs from his opening view.
evidence: Check the Agent's final response. It should report that Jones reversed his initial position to conclude a corrective JE was needed (or similar).

title: The Agent reports that the triage entry referenced the wrong GL account (131000 Prepaid Software Subscriptions) versus the real exception's account (132000 Prepaid Facilities Maintenance).
justification: The prompt says 'some of the detail on it doesn't even line up with what I'd expect.' One specific mismatch the user must be told about is the GL account: the triage entry stored 131000 Prepaid Software Subscriptions while the underlying FX drift exception is on 132000 Prepaid Facilities Maintenance.
evidence: Check the Agent's final response. It should identify that the triage entry's GL account (131000 Prepaid Software Subscriptions) did not match the real exception's account (132000 Prepaid Facilities Maintenance).

title: The Agent reports that the triage entry referenced the wrong reconciliation (archived BL-3E4ED5B5B9BA) versus the real exception's reconciliation (BL-3978BDB68290).
justification: A separate mismatch on the same triage entry is the reconciliation reference: it points to BL-3E4ED5B5B9BA (an archived FP-2025-09 recon on account 131000) instead of the actual BL-3978BDB68290 (FP-2026-05 on account 132000). This is the second piece of the 'doesn't line up' picture the user needs.
evidence: Check the Agent's final response. It should identify that the triage entry's recon_id (archived BL-3E4ED5B5B9BA) did not match the real exception's reconciliation (BL-3978BDB68290).

title: The Agent reports that the agreed resolution is a corrective JE in FP-2026-06 (DR 132000 Prepaid Facilities Maintenance $435.41 / CR FX Translation Gain $435.41).
justification: The prompt asks the Agent to 'work out the real position.' The settled resolution everyone landed on is a corrective JE for FP-2026-06 (DR 132000 Prepaid Facilities Maintenance $435.41 / CR FX Translation Gain $435.41), as set out in Harry's June 3 approval-request email.
evidence: Check the Agent's final response. It should report that the settled resolution is a corrective JE for FP-2026-06: DR 132000 Prepaid Facilities Maintenance $435.41 / CR FX Translation Gain $435.41.

title: The Agent reports that Ben Arinzo confirmed the schedule side of the corrective JE approach (that booking DR 132000 / CR FX Translation Gain $435.41 in FP-2026-06 will let the prepaid amortization schedule pick up the revised carry value).
justification: The prompt asks the Agent to 'work out the real position.' One element of the real position is that Ben Arinzo, from the prepaid schedule side, signed off on the corrective JE shape. This is a single attribution claim sourced from either Ben's threaded Slack reply on 2026-06-03 in #monthly-close-coordination or Harry's June 3 approval-request email body to James, which restates Ben's position.
evidence: Check the Agent's final response. It should report that Ben Arinzo confirmed the schedule shape / JE structure (or similar). Source may be Ben's Slack reply or Harry's June 3 email body - either is acceptable evidence.

title: The Agent reports that Hannah Grant confirmed the tax view that the May revaluation is a non-cash translation gain only with no federal deduction-timing implication.
justification: The prompt asks the Agent to 'work out the real position.' Another element of the real position is that Hannah Grant, from the tax side, signed off that the May revaluation is a non-cash translation gain only and does not create a federal deduction-timing issue. This is a single attribution claim sourced from either Hannah's standalone C005 channel post on 2026-06-03 (~2 minutes after Ben's reply) or Harry's June 3 approval-request email body to James, which restates Hannah's position.
evidence: Check the Agent's final response. It should report that Hannah Grant confirmed the tax view - non-cash translation gain only, no federal deduction-timing implication (or similar). Source may be Hannah's standalone C005 Slack post or Harry's June 3 email body - either is acceptable evidence.

title: The Agent reports that James Randall's partner sign-off is the only outstanding gate on the corrective JE.
justification: The prompt asks the Agent to 'work out the real position' and to make sure George can 'get it over the line.' With Jones, Ben, and Hannah aligned on the corrective JE, the sole remaining blocker is James Randall's sign-off - that is the actionable single-step detail George needs.
evidence: Check the Agent's final response. It should report that James Randall's partner sign-off is the only outstanding step on the corrective JE (or similar).

title: The Agent reports that the June 4 SLA on the exception has already lapsed.
justification: The SLA having lapsed is the urgency signal the user implicitly cares about (the prompt frames the blocker as 'quietly rolls into another period'). George needs to know the SLA has already passed so he understands the chase is overdue.
evidence: Check the Agent's final response. It should report that the June 4 SLA on the exception has already lapsed (or similar).

title: The Agent ties the triage entry to the correct underlying exception by relying on the exception_id (exc_cb0a9a94a3084c) rather than the mislabeled gl_account, owner, recon_id, and dates listed on the Airtable record.
justification: The triage entry's gl_account ('131000 Prepaid Software Subscriptions'), recon_id ('BL-3E4ED5B5B9BA'), owner ('George McAdam'), and created/target dates (May 5/6) all contradict the real exception (account 132000 Prepaid Facilities Maintenance, recon BL-3978BDB68290, assignee Harry Marks, logged June 2). The only correct join key is exception_id. An Outcome rubric can only verify that the agent wrote the right values in the end; it cannot confirm the agent did the disambiguation work that gets to those values legitimately (an agent could coincidentally write correct values). This process behavior is required by every valid path - any agent that trusts the Airtable fields instead of the exception_id will solve the wrong scenario.
evidence: Check the Agent's trajectory and reasoning. The Agent should identify that the Airtable fields conflict with the actual exception and explicitly use the exception_id exc_cb0a9a94a3084c as the authoritative tie to BlackLine, ignoring the mislabeled account/recon/owner/dates on the triage record.
